summary: High CPU utilization impacting API latency
root_cause: Runaway process in the data ingestion pipeline consuming excessive threads.
resolution: Restarted the data-ingestion service and applied a patch to limit thread pool size.

summary: Memory leak in user profile microservice
root_cause: Improper caching strategy caused objects to retain in heap memory linearly.
resolution: Performed a rolling restart of the profile service to clear memory. Scheduled code fix for cache eviction.

summary: Database connection pool exhaustion leading to 500 errors
root_cause: Application not releasing connections back to the pool properly during high concurrency.
resolution: Scaled up the database read replicas and increased connection pool max size.

summary: Frontend static assets incorrectly cached causing 404s
root_cause: CDN cache invalidation failed after deployment.
resolution: Purged CDN cache and rolled back to previous frontend build.

summary: Service totally unresponsive
root_cause: Load balancer misconfiguration dropped all health checks.
resolution: Corrected LB health check path.

summary: Redis Cache Stampede causing DB spike
root_cause: Redis keys expired simultaneously, causing massive concurrent DB reads.
resolution: Implemented jitter in expiration times and warmed up cache.

summary: Disk Space Exhaustion on Log Server
root_cause: Debug logging left enabled in production filled up /var/log.
resolution: Rotated logs, deleted old archives, and disabled debug mode.
logs_signature: Disk Usage > 98%, 'No space left on device'

summary: Kafka Consumer Lag increasing due to large message size
root_cause: Producer sending messages >10MB, blocking consumers.
resolution: Increased consumer fetch.max.bytes and max.partition.fetch.bytes.

summary: Kubernetes Pod CrashLoopBackOff after config change
root_cause: Typo in environment variable name in deployment YAML.
resolution: corrected environment variable key.

summary: Deadlock in PostgreSQL Database
root_cause: Two transactions updating rows in reverse order.
resolution: Killed blocking pid and refactored prompt application logic.

summary: API Rate Limiting false positives
root_cause: Rate limiter Redis node failed, falling back to local memory with lower limits.
resolution: Restored Redis connection.
logs_signature: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests, 'Redis Connection Error'

summary: DNS Resolution Latency Spikes
root_cause: CoreDNS pods overloaded on the node.
resolution: Scaled CoreDNS autoscaler min replicas.

summary: Elasticsearch Cluster Yellow Status
root_cause: Unassigned shards due to disk watermark threshold reached.
resolution: Added storage nodes and triggered shard reroute.
logs_signature: ES Status Yellow, 'flood stage disk watermark'

summary: Slow SQL Queries impacting reporting dashboard
root_cause: Missing index on 'created_at' column in large table.
resolution: Added missing index concurrently.
logs_signature: Slow Query Log > 10s, 'Seq Scan on Orders'

summary: Payments Gateway SSL Certificate Expired
root_cause: Auto-renewal cron job failed due to permission error.
resolution: Manually renewed cert and fixed cron permissions.

summary: Intermittent Packet Loss between App and DB
root_cause: Flapping network interface on the database switch.
resolution: Network team replaced the faulty switch port.

summary: High Disk I/O Wait causing slow startups
root_cause: Noisy neighbor on the shared virtualization host.
resolution: Migrated VM to a dedicated host.

summary: Payment API 3rd Party Failure
root_cause: Upstream provider Stripe/PayPal outage.
resolution: Enabled circuit breaker pattern to fail fast and queue payments.

summary: Java Heap Space Out of Memory
root_cause: Memory leak in report generation module.
resolution: Captured Heap Dump and Restarted Service.

summary: Zombie Processes consuming PID limit
root_cause: Parent process crashing without reaping children.
resolution: Manually killed zombie processes and patched parent service.
logs_signature: Running PIDs > 30000, 'fork: retry: Resource temporarily unavailable'

summary: Nginx 413 Request Entity Too Large
root_cause: Client trying to upload 50MB file when limit is 10MB.
resolution: Updated Nginx client_max_body_size config.
logs_signature: HTTP 413, 'client intended to send too large body'
